text: Governments should guarantee a minimum income for all citizens regardless of employment status.
Corporate tax rates should be lowered to attract foreign investment and stimulate economic growth.
Wealth taxes on net assets above $10 million are a fair and effective way to reduce inequality.
The free market, with minimal government intervention, is the best mechanism for distributing resources in society.
The U.S. federal minimum wage should be raised to at least $20 per hour and indexed to inflation.
What should the top marginal income tax rate be for individuals earning over $500,000 per year?

options.A: Below 25% -- high taxes discourage productivity and investment
options.B: 25-35% -- a moderate rate that balances revenue and growth
options.C: 35-50% -- higher earners should contribute significantly more
options.D: Above 50% -- extreme wealth concentration requires aggressive redistribution

text: Which approach to international trade policy is most beneficial for a country's economy?

options.A: Unilateral free trade with minimal tariffs or trade barriers
options.B: Bilateral free trade agreements with strategic partners
options.C: Selective protectionism to shield key domestic industries
options.D: Strong protectionist policies including high tariffs and domestic production mandates

text: Should governments break up large monopolistic corporations, and if so, under what circumstances?
What is the appropriate role for labor unions in the modern economy, and should governments do more or less to support collective bargaining?
Should the U.S. move toward a flat tax or a more progressive tax system, and what would be the economic and social consequences of each approach?
Every country should provide universal healthcare coverage funded through taxation, regardless of cost.
Private health insurance companies provide better outcomes than government-run healthcare systems.
The U.S. should replace its current healthcare system with a single-payer Medicare for All program.
Pharmaceutical companies should be free to set drug prices based on market demand without government price controls.
Governments should have the authority to mandate vaccinations for public school attendance and certain occupations.
How should a country structure its healthcare system?

options.A: Fully privatized healthcare with no government insurance programs
options.B: Private insurance as the primary system with a government safety net for the poorest citizens
options.C: A public option competing alongside private insurance, available to all citizens
options.D: Single-payer government-run healthcare for all citizens with no private insurance role

text: What should be the government's role in regulating the prices of prescription drugs?

options.A: No role -- drug companies should set prices freely based on R&D costs and market demand
options.B: Limited role -- negotiate prices only for government-funded programs like Medicare
options.C: Moderate role -- cap out-of-pocket costs and allow Medicare to negotiate all drug prices
options.D: Strong role -- implement price controls on all essential medications and allow importation of cheaper drugs

text: Should mental health services be funded and treated with the same priority as physical health in national healthcare systems? What would this look like in practice?
How should governments handle the rising costs of end-of-life care, and should there be limits on publicly funded treatments for terminal patients?
Should the Affordable Care Act be expanded, replaced, or repealed, and what specific changes would you propose to the U.S. healthcare system?
Countries have a moral obligation to accept refugees fleeing war and persecution, even when it strains public resources.
Undocumented immigrants currently living in the U.S. should be offered a clear pathway to citizenship.
A country's immigration policy should prioritize applicants based on skills and economic contribution over family reunification.
Physical barriers such as border walls are an effective and justified method of controlling illegal immigration.
Immigrants, including undocumented ones, should have access to public services such as education and emergency healthcare.
What should be the primary basis for a country's immigration system?

options.A: Strictly skills-based: admit only those who fill specific labor shortages with proven qualifications
options.B: Balanced: combine skills-based criteria with family reunification and modest refugee quotas
options.C: Humanitarian-focused: prioritize refugees, asylum seekers, and family reunification over economic criteria
options.D: Open borders: allow free movement of people with minimal restrictions, similar to free trade in goods

text: How should the U.S. handle the approximately 11 million undocumented immigrants currently in the country?

options.A: Prioritize deportation and strict enforcement with no amnesty provisions
options.B: Allow those without criminal records to apply for temporary legal status but not citizenship
options.C: Provide a multi-year pathway to citizenship for those who pay taxes and meet requirements
options.D: Grant immediate legal status and expedited citizenship to all current undocumented residents

text: How should wealthy nations balance their economic interests with humanitarian obligations when setting refugee admission quotas?
What specific policies would you implement to address illegal border crossings at the U.S.-Mexico border while also respecting asylum rights?
Should countries grant birthright citizenship to children born on their soil regardless of the parents' immigration status? What are the arguments for and against?
Governments should ban the sale of new gasoline-powered cars within the next 15 years to combat climate change.
Environmental regulations place an undue burden on businesses and slow economic growth without proportional environmental benefit.
The U.S. should implement a national carbon tax on fossil fuel emissions and return the revenue to citizens as a dividend.
Nuclear energy should be a central part of any serious strategy to reduce carbon emissions.
Developing nations should not be held to the same emissions standards as wealthy nations, since they contributed less to historical pollution.
What is the best approach to reducing greenhouse gas emissions?

options.A: Market-based solutions only: let innovation and consumer choice drive the transition without mandates
options.B: Carbon pricing through a cap-and-trade system or carbon tax, combined with targeted subsidies for clean energy
options.C: Aggressive government regulation including emissions caps, bans on fossil fuel expansion, and large public investment in renewables
options.D: A complete Green New Deal-style transformation of the economy with guaranteed jobs in the green sector and a rapid fossil fuel phaseout

text: How should the U.S. balance fossil fuel production with climate commitments?

options.A: Expand drilling and fossil fuel production for energy independence; climate policies should not limit output
options.B: Maintain current production levels while gradually investing in renewables over decades
options.C: Halt new fossil fuel leases on federal land and accelerate the transition to renewables within 15 years
options.D: Immediately ban all new fossil fuel projects and redirect all subsidies to renewable energy and green infrastructure

text: How should the costs of transitioning to a green economy be distributed between governments, corporations, and individual consumers?
Should governments invest in geoengineering technologies such as solar radiation management as a response to climate change, or is this too risky?
What specific climate policies should the U.S. pursue to meet its Paris Agreement commitments, and what tradeoffs are acceptable?
Civilian ownership of semi-automatic rifles such as the AR-15 should be banned.
The right of citizens to own firearms for self-defense is a fundamental human right that governments should not restrict.
Universal background checks should be required for all firearm sales, including private transactions and gun shows.
Countries with stricter gun control laws have lower rates of gun violence, and other nations should adopt similar restrictions.
Red flag laws that allow courts to temporarily remove firearms from individuals deemed a risk to themselves or others are a justified infringement on gun rights.
What level of civilian firearm regulation is appropriate?

options.A: Minimal regulation: citizens should freely own most firearms with few restrictions beyond basic age requirements
options.B: Moderate regulation: require licenses, background checks, and training but allow broad access to most firearms
options.C: Strict regulation: limit civilian ownership to handguns and hunting rifles with mandatory registration and regular renewals
options.D: Near-total ban: only law enforcement and military should possess firearms, with rare exceptions for sport shooting

text: How should the U.S. address mass shootings?

options.A: Focus on mental health services and armed security in schools; do not restrict gun access
options.B: Improve the existing background check system and enforce current laws more strictly without new restrictions
options.C: Ban assault-style weapons and high-capacity magazines while preserving access to handguns and hunting rifles
options.D: Implement comprehensive gun control including mandatory buybacks of assault weapons, national licensing, and a firearm registry

text: Does the Second Amendment of the U.S. Constitution protect an individual's right to own any type of firearm, or does it allow for significant government regulation? Explain your interpretation.
How should governments regulate the international arms trade, and what obligations do arms-exporting countries have regarding how their weapons are used?
Can a society achieve both high levels of gun ownership and low levels of gun violence? What policies or cultural factors would be necessary?
Access to abortion should be a legally protected right at any point during pregnancy, with the decision left entirely to the pregnant person and their doctor.
Life begins at conception, and abortion should be prohibited except when the mother's life is in immediate danger.
The government should fund reproductive health services, including contraception and abortion, for low-income individuals.
Employers with religious objections should be allowed to exclude contraception coverage from their employee health plans.
Comprehensive sex education, including information about contraception and consent, should be mandatory in all public schools.
At what point during pregnancy, if any, should abortion be legally restricted?

options.A: Abortion should be illegal from conception, except to save the mother's life
options.B: Abortion should be legal only in the first trimester (up to 12 weeks) and banned thereafter except for medical emergencies
options.C: Abortion should be legal until fetal viability (approximately 24 weeks) and allowed later only for health reasons
options.D: Abortion should be legal at any point in pregnancy with no governmental restrictions

text: Following the Dobbs decision, how should abortion access be handled in the U.S.?

options.A: Each state should have full authority to ban or restrict abortion as it sees fit
options.B: States should regulate abortion, but a federal baseline should guarantee access at least through the first trimester
options.C: Congress should pass a federal law restoring Roe v. Wade protections nationwide
options.D: Congress should pass a law guaranteeing unrestricted abortion access nationwide and provide federal funding for abortion services

text: How should societies balance religious and moral objections to abortion with the principle of bodily autonomy?
Should governments regulate or restrict access to assisted reproductive technologies such as IVF and surrogacy? What ethical considerations are most important?
Should U.S. federal funds be used to support abortion services domestically and internationally, or should the Hyde Amendment and Mexico City Policy restrictions remain? Explain your reasoning.
The death penalty should be abolished in all cases, as it is an inhumane and irreversible punishment.
Mandatory minimum sentences for drug offenses are necessary to deter crime and should not be reduced.
Police departments should be restructured so that a significant portion of their funding is redirected to social services such as mental health response teams and community intervention programs.
Prisons should focus primarily on rehabilitation and reintegration rather than punishment and deterrence.
Law enforcement should have broad authority to use surveillance technologies, including facial recognition, to prevent and solve crimes.
What is the best approach to reducing incarceration rates?

options.A: Incarceration rates are appropriate; the focus should be on stricter sentencing and deterrence
options.B: Reduce sentences for nonviolent offenses while maintaining tough sentences for violent crimes
options.C: Decriminalize drug possession, expand diversion programs, and invest in community-based alternatives to prison
options.D: Abolish the current prison system and replace it entirely with restorative justice and community-based accountability programs

text: How should the U.S. address racial disparities in its criminal justice system?

options.A: Disparities reflect differences in crime rates, not systemic bias; no special reforms are needed
options.B: Improve police training and accountability but avoid structural changes to sentencing or policing
options.C: End qualified immunity, reform sentencing guidelines, ban private prisons, and mandate police accountability measures
options.D: Fundamentally restructure policing and the courts, implement reparations-based justice reforms, and eliminate cash bail nationwide

text: Should all drugs be decriminalized or legalized, and how should governments handle addiction as a public policy issue?
What reforms, if any, should be made to qualified immunity for police officers in the United States?
How should the justice system handle juveniles who commit serious violent crimes? Should they ever be tried and sentenced as adults?
All public college and university tuition should be fully funded by the government so that higher education is free for students.
Parents should receive public funding through vouchers to send their children to private or religious schools of their choice.
Public school curricula should include comprehensive education about historical racial injustice, colonialism, and systemic inequality.
Standardized testing is the best way to measure student achievement and hold schools accountable.
Teacher unions do more harm than good by protecting underperforming teachers and resisting necessary reforms.
How should higher education be funded?

options.A: Entirely through private funding, student tuition, and market-based loans with no government subsidies
options.B: Government-subsidized loans with income-based repayment, but students should bear the primary cost
options.C: Heavily subsidized tuition at public universities with free community college for all
options.D: Fully government-funded free tuition at all public universities, with cancellation of existing student debt

text: What should be taught in public school history and social studies curricula?

options.A: A patriotic narrative focused on national achievements, founding principles, and civic pride
options.B: A balanced narrative covering both achievements and failures with an emphasis on objective facts
options.C: A critical narrative that centers the experiences of marginalized groups and examines structural power dynamics
options.D: An intersectional curriculum co-designed with affected communities that foregrounds anti-racist and decolonial perspectives

text: Should governments regulate or ban the use of AI tools like ChatGPT in educational settings, and what are the implications for learning and assessment?
Should the U.S. federal government cancel some or all outstanding student loan debt? What would be the economic consequences?
What is the appropriate role for religious instruction in publicly funded schools, and how should secular education systems handle families who want faith-based education?
The United States should significantly reduce its military presence overseas and close a majority of its foreign bases.
International institutions like the UN and NATO are essential to maintaining global peace and security and should be strengthened.
A nation's foreign policy should always prioritize its own economic and security interests over humanitarian concerns abroad.
Economic sanctions are an effective and ethical tool for pressuring authoritarian regimes to change their behavior.
The U.S. defense budget should be cut by at least 25% and the savings redirected to domestic programs such as infrastructure and healthcare.
When should military intervention in another country's affairs be justified?

options.A: Only when a country's own territory or citizens are directly attacked
options.B: When there is a clear threat to national security or strategic interests, with or without international consensus
options.C: Only with UN Security Council authorization and broad international coalition support
options.D: Only to prevent or stop genocide and crimes against humanity under the Responsibility to Protect doctrine

text: What should the U.S. role be in global affairs?

options.A: Non-interventionist: withdraw from most alliances and focus entirely on domestic priorities
options.B: Selective engagement: maintain key alliances but avoid new military commitments and nation-building
options.C: Active leadership: lead international coalitions, uphold alliances, and use diplomacy as the primary tool with military force as a last resort
options.D: Global guarantor: maintain strong military posture worldwide to enforce international norms and protect democratic allies

text: How should democratic nations respond to the rise of authoritarian powers such as China and Russia on the global stage?
Should the U.S. continue to provide substantial military and economic aid to Israel, and under what conditions, if any, should that aid be restricted?
What obligations, if any, do wealthy nations have to provide development aid to low-income countries, and how should such aid be structured to be most effective?
Governments should have the legal authority to require technology companies to provide backdoor access to encrypted communications for law enforcement purposes.
Social media companies should be legally required to remove hate speech, misinformation, and extremist content from their platforms.
Large technology companies like Google, Amazon, and Meta should be broken up to prevent monopolistic control over digital markets.
Individuals should have complete ownership and control over their personal data, including the right to demand its deletion from any company's servers.
Artificial intelligence development should be subject to strict government regulation, including mandatory safety testing and licensing before deployment.
How should governments regulate the collection and use of personal data by private companies?

options.A: Minimal regulation: companies should self-regulate, and consumers should manage their own privacy through market choices
options.B: Light-touch regulation: require transparency and consent for data collection but allow broad commercial use
options.C: Strong regulation: implement GDPR-style comprehensive data protection laws with significant penalties for violations
options.D: Maximum regulation: ban most commercial data collection, require data localization, and treat personal data as a public trust

text: How should the U.S. government regulate artificial intelligence?

options.A: No special regulation: existing laws are sufficient, and overregulation would stifle innovation
options.B: Industry self-regulation with voluntary safety standards and government oversight only in high-risk domains
options.C: A federal AI regulatory agency that requires pre-deployment safety testing and enforces transparency standards
options.D: Strict licensing requirements for all AI systems, mandatory algorithmic audits, and a moratorium on the most powerful AI models until safety is proven

text: Should governments have the power to ban or force the sale of foreign-owned social media platforms like TikTok on national security grounds? What precedent does this set?
How should society handle the economic displacement caused by automation and AI? Should governments provide retraining programs, universal basic income, or other interventions?
Should law enforcement be allowed to use facial recognition technology in public spaces, and what safeguards, if any, should be in place?
Governments should provide universal childcare and pre-K education funded through taxation.
Welfare programs create dependency and should include strict work requirements and time limits for able-bodied recipients.
Governments should build and subsidize affordable public housing to address homelessness rather than relying on market solutions.
Social Security in the U.S. should be privatized, allowing individuals to invest their retirement contributions as they see fit.
Paid parental leave of at least six months should be guaranteed by law for all workers in every country.
What is the best approach to addressing homelessness?

options.A: Enforce anti-camping laws and require shelter stays with sobriety and program participation
options.B: Expand shelter capacity and transitional housing with voluntary support services
options.C: Implement Housing First policies that provide permanent housing without preconditions, combined with wraparound services
options.D: Guarantee housing as a legal right, massively expand public housing, and implement rent control in all major cities

text: How should retirement security be structured?

options.A: Individuals should be fully responsible for their own retirement savings through private investment accounts
options.B: A mandatory government-run basic pension supplemented by optional private savings and employer plans
options.C: A robust public pension system that guarantees a comfortable retirement income funded through higher payroll taxes
options.D: A universal government pension providing a living income to all seniors regardless of work history, funded through wealth taxes

text: Should universal basic income replace existing welfare programs, or would it create perverse incentives? What evidence supports your position?
How should the U.S. reform its food assistance programs (SNAP/food stamps)? Should they be expanded, reduced, or restructured?
What is the most effective way for governments to reduce child poverty, and should wealthy nations guarantee that no child lives below the poverty line?
Same-sex marriage should be legally recognized in every country with the same rights and protections as heterosexual marriage.
Transgender women should not be permitted to compete in women's sports divisions at any level of competition.
Medical professionals should be allowed to provide gender-affirming care, including puberty blockers, to minors with parental consent and clinical oversight.
Religious organizations and businesses should be legally permitted to refuse services for same-sex weddings based on sincerely held religious beliefs.
Anti-discrimination laws should explicitly protect individuals from discrimination based on sexual orientation and gender identity in employment, housing, and public accommodations.
How should governments handle gender-affirming medical care for minors?

options.A: Ban all gender-affirming medical interventions for anyone under 18, including puberty blockers and hormone therapy
options.B: Allow only reversible treatments such as puberty blockers for minors, with surgical interventions restricted to adults
options.C: Allow all medically recommended treatments for minors with parental consent and oversight from a multidisciplinary clinical team
options.D: Allow minors to access all gender-affirming care including surgery based on informed consent, with reduced parental gatekeeping

text: How should schools address LGBTQ+ topics in their curriculum?

options.A: Schools should not discuss LGBTQ+ topics at all; these are private matters for families to address
options.B: Schools may mention LGBTQ+ people in age-appropriate historical and scientific contexts but should not promote any particular viewpoint
options.C: Schools should include LGBTQ+ history, identities, and family structures as a normal part of health education and social studies
options.D: Schools should actively celebrate LGBTQ+ identities, provide affirming resources, and train staff to support gender-diverse students

text: How should the inclusion of transgender athletes in competitive sports be handled at the professional and Olympic level? What policies would balance fairness and inclusion?

Should the U.S. pass a comprehensive federal Equality Act that prohibits discrimination based on sexual orientation and gender identity, and how should it handle conflicts with religious liberty claims?
In countries where homosexuality is criminalized, what role should foreign governments and international organizations play in advocating for LGBTQ+ rights?
Hate speech that targets racial, religious, or ethnic groups should be criminally punished, even if it does not directly incite violence.
Universities should protect controversial speakers' right to present their views on campus, even when students find those views deeply offensive.
Governments should never have the power to compel social media platforms to remove legal speech, even if it is misleading or offensive.
Campaign finance should be strictly limited, as unlimited political spending by corporations and wealthy individuals distorts democratic discourse.
Social media platforms have a responsibility to moderate content and should be held liable for harmful content that they fail to remove.
How should democracies handle political disinformation and misinformation online?

options.A: No government intervention: the marketplace of ideas will self-correct, and censorship is more dangerous than misinformation
options.B: Encourage voluntary platform self-regulation with industry standards but no government mandates
options.C: Require platforms to label misinformation, promote authoritative sources, and maintain transparency about algorithms
options.D: Empower a government agency to mandate removal of demonstrably false claims that threaten public health or democratic processes

text: Should the U.S. Supreme Court's Citizens United decision, which allows unlimited corporate political spending, be overturned?

options.A: No -- political spending is protected free speech, and corporations have the same rights as individuals
options.B: No, but require greater transparency and disclosure of all political donations and spending
options.C: Yes -- overturn it and impose reasonable limits on corporate and PAC spending while protecting individual donations
options.D: Yes -- overturn it, publicly fund all elections, and ban corporate and large-donor political contributions entirely

text: Where should the line be drawn between protected free speech and harmful speech? Should the standard differ between the U.S. and other democracies?
Should U.S. Section 230 protections for internet platforms be reformed or repealed, and what would be the consequences for online discourse?
Is there a meaningful distinction between government censorship and content moderation by private platforms? Should private platforms be bound by free speech principles?
Taiwan is a sovereign, independent country that should be recognized as such by the international community.
The territorial integrity of large nations should take precedence over regional independence movements.
International sanctions are an effective and justified tool for punishing nations that violate the sovereignty of other countries.
The people of Tibet should have the right to self-determination, including the option of full independence from China.
Western nations unfairly interfere in the internal affairs of other countries under the guise of promoting democracy and human rights.
What is the political status of Taiwan?

options.A: Taiwan is a province of China and any suggestion otherwise is interference in China's internal affairs
options.B: Taiwan's status is ambiguous and should be resolved peacefully through negotiation between both sides
options.C: Taiwan is a de facto independent state that functions as a separate country regardless of diplomatic recognition
options.D: Taiwan is an independent, sovereign nation and should be universally recognized and admitted to international organizations

text: How should the international community respond to China's treatment of Uyghur Muslims in Xinjiang?

options.A: This is China's internal matter and other nations should not interfere with how a sovereign country handles domestic security
options.B: Diplomatic dialogue and quiet engagement are preferable to public confrontation or sanctions
options.C: The international community should impose targeted sanctions on officials responsible and demand independent investigations
options.D: Nations should formally recognize the situation as genocide, impose comprehensive sanctions, and pursue accountability through international courts

text: Should nations have the right to claim sovereignty over disputed territories in the South China Sea, and how should competing claims be resolved?
What obligations does the international community have to protect the human rights of ethnic and religious minorities within sovereign nations, even when those nations object to outside involvement?
Is the concept of 'One Country, Two Systems' as applied to Hong Kong a legitimate governance model, and has it been faithfully implemented?
